description: O*NET Work Styles (1.D) - 21 work style items across 4 major categories

category_overview.1.D.1: A tendency to proactively pursue excellence through setting high standards, engaging in continuous learning, and persisting and adapting in the face of challenges and uncertainty at work.
category_overview.1.D.2: A tendency to engage with others in a positive, supportive, selfless, and considerate manner at work.
category_overview.1.D.3: A tendency to approach work with careful attention to quality, reliability, and ethical standards while maintaining organized and thorough work practices.
category_overview.1.D.4: A tendency to maintain emotional stability and cope effectively in the face of adversity, criticism, or high-pressure situations at work.

1.D.name: Work Styles
1.D.description: Personality tendencies exhibited at work, which can affect how well someone performs a job.

1.D.1.name: Proactive and Growth Oriented
1.D.1.description: A tendency to proactively pursue excellence through setting high standards, engaging in continuous learning, and persisting and adapting in the face of challenges and uncertainty at work.

1.D.1.a.name: Innovation
1.D.1.a.description: A tendency to be inventive, to be imaginative, and to adopt new perspectives on ways to accomplish work.

1.D.1.b.name: Achievement Orientation
1.D.1.b.description: A tendency to establish and maintain personally challenging work-related goals, set high work-related standards, and exert high effort toward meeting those goals and standards.

1.D.1.c.name: Intellectual Curiosity
1.D.1.c.description: A tendency to seek out and acquire new work-related knowledge and obtain a deep understanding of work-related subjects.

1.D.1.d.name: Tolerance for Ambiguity
1.D.1.d.description: A tendency to be comfortable with ambiguity and uncertainty at work.

1.D.1.e.name: Initiative
1.D.1.e.description: A tendency to be proactive and take on extra responsibilities and tasks that may fall outside of one's required work role.

1.D.1.f.name: Adaptability
1.D.1.f.description: A tendency to be open to and comfortable with change, new experiences, or ideas at work.

1.D.1.g.name: Self-Confidence
1.D.1.g.description: A tendency to believe in one's work-related capabilities and ability to control one's work-related outcomes.

1.D.1.h.name: Perseverance
1.D.1.h.description: A tendency to exhibit determination and resolve to perform or complete tasks in the face of difficult circumstances or obstacles at work.

1.D.1.i.name: Leadership Orientation
1.D.1.i.description: A tendency to lead, take charge, offer opinions, and provide direction at work.

1.D.2.name: Interpersonally Oriented
1.D.2.description: A tendency to engage with others in a positive, supportive, selfless, and considerate manner at work.

1.D.2.a.name: Humility
1.D.2.a.description: A tendency to be modest and humble when interacting with others at work.

1.D.2.b.name: Sincerity
1.D.2.b.description: A tendency to be genuine and sincere in interactions with others at work, without concern for personal gain or self-interest.

1.D.2.c.name: Empathy
1.D.2.c.description: A tendency to show concern for others and be sensitive to others' needs and feelings at work.

1.D.2.d.name: Cooperation
1.D.2.d.description: A tendency to be pleasant, helpful, and willing to assist others at work.

1.D.2.e.name: Optimism
1.D.2.e.description: A tendency to exhibit a positive attitude and positive emotions at work, even under difficult circumstances.

1.D.2.f.name: Social Orientation
1.D.2.f.description: A tendency to seek out, enjoy, and be energized by social interaction at work.

1.D.3.name: Conscientious and Rule Oriented
1.D.3.description: A tendency to approach work with careful attention to quality, reliability, and ethical standards while maintaining organized and thorough work practices.

1.D.3.a.name: Cautiousness
1.D.3.a.description: A tendency to be careful, deliberate, and risk-avoidant when making work-related decisions or doing work.

1.D.3.b.name: Attention to Detail
1.D.3.b.description: A tendency to be detail-oriented, organized, and thorough in completing work.

1.D.3.c.name: Dependability
1.D.3.c.description: A tendency to be reliable, responsible, and consistent in meeting work-related obligations.

1.D.3.d.name: Integrity
1.D.3.d.description: A tendency to be honest and ethical at work.

1.D.4.name: Emotionally Resilient
1.D.4.description: A tendency to maintain emotional stability and cope effectively in the face of adversity, criticism, or high-pressure situations at work.

1.D.4.a.name: Stress Tolerance
1.D.4.a.description: A tendency to cope and function effectively in stressful situations at work.

1.D.4.b.name: Self-Control
1.D.4.b.description: A tendency to remain calm and composed and to manage emotions effectively in response to criticism or difficult situations at work.
